top_2_bugs.issue: Immutability violation in render scroll loop — lines 350-355 mutate ScrollingWord.y and .fadeAlpha directly on objects inside the array, which violates the interface comment ('mutated only locally in render loop') but more critically bypasses any future freeze/proxy guards and makes the mutation invisible to diffing
top_2_bugs.one_line_fix: Replace the for-of mutation loop with: this.scrollingWords = this.scrollingWords.map(sw => sw.picked ? { ...sw, y: sw.y - this.scrollSpeed * dt, fadeAlpha: sw.fadeAlpha - dt * 3 } : { ...sw, y: sw.y - this.scrollSpeed * dt });

top_2_bugs.issue: spawnWord pre-seed passes startY = Math.random() * canvasH * 0.8 (line 206), which allows startY values near 0 — words spawned at y < wordHeight/2 are immediately clipped by the top-fade overlay (y <= 40) and removed on the very next filter pass (y > -20), wasting one of the 8 pre-seeded slots before the player can ever see or click them
top_2_bugs.one_line_fix: Change to: this.spawnWord(this.wordHeight + Math.random() * (this.canvasH * 0.8 - this.wordHeight)); to guarantee all pre-seeded words start below the top-fade zone.

pointerleave_pointercancel.note: Both events registered in setup() (lines 118-119) and handler sets cursorX/Y to -1 (lines 160-163), which places cursor 269px from center — well outside targetRadius 18-30px. isOnTarget recomputes false on the very next render tick. No stuck-cursor risk.

endGame_double_guard.note: this.ended flag checked at line 207 before any side effects. Both the timer callback (line 147) and any future caller are guarded. Idempotent.

cancelTimers_contract.note: endGame() clears both timerInterval (clearInterval) and animFrame (cancelAnimationFrame) at lines 209-210. cleanup() mirrors this at lines 380-381. No cancelTimers() abstraction needed; direct calls are consistent across both exit paths.

sfx_during_gameplay.note: Edge-triggered SFX on target enter ('unlock') and leave ('lose') inside render(), guarded by wasOnTarget/isOnTarget diff. critical_alert SFX at 3s via checkCriticalAlert() in timer (line 140). No SFX fired when ended is true because render() stops being scheduled after endGame() cancels animFrame.

difficultyTier_used.note: All four config params (totalTime, targetRadius, spawnInterval, maxDistractors) are tier-scaled via tieredValue() in setup(). totalTime is correctly non-readonly (C103 fix documented at line 38).

score_formula.note: score = (timeOnTarget / totalTime) * 100. Uses totalTime (post-scaling) as denominator, so score is normalized correctly for all tiers. Denominator zero-guarded at line 217.

spawnWord_preseed_waste.detail: Pre-seed loop calls spawnWord(Math.random() * this.canvasH * 0.8). With canvasH=400, startY range is [0, 320]. Values below ~42 (wordHeight/2 + top-fade zone 40px) are immediately masked by the top-fade overlay and filtered out on the first render pass (y > -20 but visually invisible). Statistically ~10% of the 8 pre-seeded words (≈1 word per run) is wasted.
spawnWord_preseed_waste.one_line_fix: Change line 206 to: this.spawnWord(this.wordHeight + Math.random() * (this.canvasH * 0.8 - this.wordHeight));

scrollingWords_mutation_loop.detail: The render loop iterates this.scrollingWords with for-of and mutates sw.y and sw.fadeAlpha in-place (lines 351, 353-354). Although ScrollingWord declares x/y/picked/fadeAlpha as non-readonly mutable fields, this pattern directly contradicts the project immutability rule (common/coding-style.md: 'ALWAYS create new objects, NEVER mutate existing ones') and the typescript/coding-style.md spread-update requirement. TypeScript does not enforce field immutability here, so the violation is silent.
scrollingWords_mutation_loop.one_line_fix: Replace lines 350-355 with: this.scrollingWords = this.scrollingWords.map(sw => ({ ...sw, y: sw.y - this.scrollSpeed * dt, fadeAlpha: sw.picked ? sw.fadeAlpha - dt * 3 : sw.fadeAlpha }));

cancelTimers_contract.note: endGame() clears both timerInterval and animFrame (lines 313-314). cleanup() mirrors at lines 493-496. Consistent with mg_volonte pattern.

sfx_during_gameplay.note: SFX dispatched on word pick in both onPointerDown (lines 293-297) and onKeyDown (lines 110-113): 'unlock' for faction words, 'lose' for neutral. critical_alert at 3s via checkCriticalAlert() in timer (line 212). Both handlers check this.ended guard (onKeyDown line 84; onPointerDown implicitly via endGame removing listener). No SFX after game ends.

difficultyTier_used.note: totalTime, scrollSpeed, and spawnInterval are tier-scaled in setup(). maxWords is readonly at 30 (intentional: bounded by canvas height, not difficulty). Correct.

score_formula.note: factionScore (max 80) + comboBonus (max 25) - penalty (neutralPicked * 3) + timeBonus (max 15) = max 120 clamped to 100. Formula is balanced: a player picking 10 faction words in a perfect combo (50 combo points) still needs the time bonus to reach 100. Penalty for neutral words is proportional. Zero-division safe (totalTime always > 0 post-tieredValue).

issues.recommendation: Use .map() with spread to produce a new array with updated y and fadeAlpha values, matching the project immutability contract.
Clamp startY lower bound to wordHeight to prevent pre-seeded words from spawning inside the top-fade masked zone.
